_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.

ssh_attacks.fail2ban_status: active - sshd jail running, 9 currently banned, 3175 total banned
ssh_attacks.change_since_last: Failed attempts at 21736 (up from 21015, +721). fail2ban total bans 3175 (up from 3067, +108). 9 IPs currently banned (up from 7). Continued botnet scanning activity at normal rate. Only authorized logins from novakj via publickey.

pdf_security.innerHTML_note: 37 usages across 12 files: viewer.js(4), upload.js(2), merge.js(4), annotate.js(2), pages.js(4), split.js(6), redact.js(3), img2pdf.js(3), text-overlay.js(2), bookmarks.js(4), ocr.js(1), crop.js(2) - all static templates, clearing operations, static SVGs, HTML entities, escaped user data (escapeHtml/escapeAttr), or integer-only interpolation. No user data injected unsafely.

crop_module_review.innerHTML_note: 2 usages: clearing canvas container (line 171), static SVG in button reset (line 738). No user data in HTML.
crop_module_review.user_data_handling: Numeric crop inputs parsed with parseFloat() and validated against page dimensions. Page numbers are integers. Unit selector uses controlled dropdown. No user strings injected into HTML. Download filename uses sanitizeFilename() via downloadBlob() from utils.js.
crop_module_review.dom_construction: All UI elements created via document.createElement() with className/style. Canvas rendering uses 2D context API only.
crop_module_review.memory_handling: Temporary canvases created for auto-trim margin detection and preview — scoped to function, garbage collected. No object URL leaks.
crop_module_review.logic_bug: Line 684: [...mb] and [...cb] uses array spread on objects from page.getMediaBox()/getCropBox(). These return plain objects, not arrays. Throws TypeError at runtime. NOT a security issue — causes feature crash, no data exposure.

bookmarks_module_review.innerHTML_note: 4 usages: clearing container (line 93), static SVG arrow (line 148), filter highlight via highlightMatch() with escapeHtml() (line 168), escapeHtml helper return (line 233). Bookmark titles displayed via textContent (line 164) by default. Filter highlight uses escapeHtml() on all text segments before wrapping match in <mark> tag.

ocr_module_review.innerHTML_note: 1 usage: static SVG icon for extract button reset (line 249). Extracted text displayed in textarea.value, stats via textContent.

img2pdf_module_review.image_validation: MIME type via ACCEPTED_TYPES Set + file extension regex. Both checked with OR logic.

redact_module_review.redaction_limitation: Draws opaque rectangles but does NOT remove underlying content streams. Hidden data may still be extractable.

signatures_module_review.fetch_data_url: Line 798 uses fetch(sig.dataURL) where dataURL is from canvas.toDataURL(). This is valid in modern browsers — Fetch API supports data: URLs. Image type validated at upload (PNG/JPEG/WebP only). Size validated (2MB limit). No security concern.

jszip_min_js.purpose: ZIP file creation for split and convert module downloads

known_issues: server_tokens set to 'build' - leaks nginx version (nginx/1.26.3 Ubuntu) in Server header. Should be 'off'.
No general *.md nginx block - only CLAUDE.md specifically blocked. Any new .md file added to webroot would be publicly accessible.
UFW firewall is inactive - only SSH and HTTP/HTTPS ports are needed but no firewall enforces this
disable_symlinks off in nginx config - low risk since only novakj can write to webroot
CGI location block exists in nginx but no CGI scripts present - dead config that could be removed
Codex proxy at /codex returns 502 - backend not running. No security risk but dead endpoint.
TASK-016 redaction tool draws opaque rectangles but does NOT strip underlying content streams - redacted text/images may still be extractable from the PDF file. Users should be warned.

img2pdf.js image validation uses OR logic (MIME type OR extension) - slightly permissive but acceptable for client-side processing since images are rendered via browser Image API which rejects non-images.
notes: Full security review completed - 41 checks passed, 0 critical security findings

NEW: Reviewed crop.js module (TASK-020): 2 innerHTML usages (clearing + static SVG), both safe. Numeric inputs parsed/validated. Download uses sanitizeFilename(). No eval/Function/document.write. XSS-safe. Logic bug on line 684 ([...mb] array spread on object) causes runtime crash — this is a functional bug, NOT a security vulnerability.
All 22 JS files scanned (20 in js/ + 2 in lib/): no eval(), Function(), document.write(), or unsafe patterns found
37 innerHTML usages across 12 files - all verified safe (up from 35/11; crop.js adds 2 safe usages)

SSH: Continued botnet scanning. Failed attempts at 21736 (+721 since last review). fail2ban total bans 3175 (+108). 9 IPs currently banned (up from 7). Only authorized logins from novakj via publickey.
SSL certificate valid until June 18, 2026 (78 days remaining)
recommendations: MEDIUM: Add user-facing warning in redaction UI that underlying PDF data may not be fully removed - only visually covered.
LOW: Set server_tokens off in nginx.conf to stop leaking version info (currently exposes nginx/1.26.3)
LOW: Enable UFW firewall (sudo ufw allow 22,80,443/tcp && sudo ufw enable)
LOW: Add general *.md deny rule to nginx with explicit exception for known safe files
LOW: Remove unused CGI location block from nginx config (no scripts exist)
LOW: Consider disable_symlinks on in nginx for defense in depth
